name: repair
author: dojo-community
context: Repair broken dependency state, pending dpkg configuration, and APT lock contention on Debian and Ubuntu hosts
info: This skill handles the failure modes agents hit after interrupted package operations, incomplete local deb installs, conflicting repositories, or concurrent package managers. It centers on `apt-get check`, `apt-get install -f`, `dpkg --configure -a`, and lock inspection rather than ad hoc file deletion. Use it whenever APT reports broken dependencies, `dpkg` is left half-configured, or package changes are blocked by another process or timer.
triggers: find what holds the apt lock

sections.title: Check dependency health
sections.body: `apt-get check` is the first pass for repair because it refreshes the cache and then validates dependency consistency. Pair it with `apt-cache unmet` when you need a faster summary of the unresolved relationships APT sees.

sections.title: Finish pending dpkg configuration
sections.body: `dpkg --configure -a` repairs the state where packages have been unpacked but not configured. This is the usual fix after interrupted installs, failed maintainer scripts, or a reboot during package work.

sections.title: Use apt-get install -f carefully
sections.body: `apt-get install -f` asks the solver to correct broken dependencies. It is the standard recovery path after a failed local deb install or a partial transaction. If the dependency graph is severely corrupted, Debian notes that manual removal of an offending package may still be required.

sections.title: Inspect lock contention first
sections.body: APT and `dpkg` use lock files to serialize package operations. A present lock file is not itself the problem; an active owner is. Identify the owning process with `lsof` or `fuser`, decide whether it is a legitimate package operation, and only then retry or stop it intentionally.

links.context: See the broader failure taxonomy and log locations
Inspect repository policy if breakage follows a source change
Audit third-party repositories if signatures or conflicting packages are involved
Resume or repeat the intended install once package state is healthy again

related.note: Troubleshooting helps classify whether the issue is broken state, lock contention, or repository trust

scripts.name: Repair APT and dpkg state
scripts.description: Check package health, finish pending config, fix broken deps, or inspect lock holders

APT_REPAIR_ACTION.description: Repair action: check, configure-pending, fix-broken, fix-missing, or locks

APT_PACKAGES.description: Package specs used only with fix-missing

APT_ASSUME_YES.description: Set to true to pass -y to apt-get where supported

APT_SIMULATE.description: Set to true to simulate apt-get actions with -s

packages.description: Package specs to use with fix-missing

body:
# Repairing Package State

Use this node when package operations have already gone wrong. Start with diagnostics and the least destructive repair path.

## Diagnostics

```bash
sudo apt-get check
apt-cache unmet
```

`apt-get check` updates the cache and verifies dependency consistency. `apt-cache unmet` gives a quick summary of unresolved dependencies visible from the metadata.

## Reconfigure pending packages

```bash
sudo dpkg --configure -a
```

Run this after interrupted package installation, reboot during package configuration, or a failed post-install script. It asks `dpkg` to finish configuring unpacked but incomplete packages.

## Fix broken dependencies

```bash
sudo apt-get install -f
```

`-f` asks APT to deduce a likely solution for broken dependencies. Debian warns that highly corrupt dependency structures may still need manual intervention, usually by removing an offending package with `dpkg`.

## Lock contention

```bash
sudo lsof /var/lib/dpkg/lock-frontend /var/lib/dpkg/lock \
  /var/lib/apt/lists/lock /var/cache/apt/archives/lock
```

Do not delete lock files blindly. Identify the owning process first. Common owners include another `apt-get`, unattended package timers, cloud-init, or package installation hooks running through a different terminal.

## Missing package downloads

If package files fail to download, `apt-get --fix-missing install ...` can hold back the failed package and continue with the rest. Use it deliberately with explicit packages, then investigate network or repository integrity separately.
